title: Bounded Post-Install Jumpstart Phase
goal: Define the first bounded post-bootstrap jumpstart phase for mature repos so safe install/adopt can deliver visible value sooner without broad repo conversion.
non_goals: Make `init` more aggressive in ambiguous repos.
Bulk-import docs into Memory or Planning.
Build a generic repo analysis pipeline or full migration flow.
Convert all mature-repo context in one pass.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: make mature-repo adoption feel useful quickly while preserving the conservative safe-install posture.

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: promote GitHub issue `#48` into an active execplan that defines the first bounded jumpstart phase for mature repos.
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: stay post-bootstrap; keep `init` conservative; avoid bulk import; keep the first slice small and reviewable; preserve explicit safety boundaries.
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: the exact surfaced name, which one or two seeded surfaces matter first, how much ranking policy belongs in this slice, and the smallest validation set needed to dogfood it.
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the slice would require widening `init`, introducing broad repository conversion semantics, or building a generic analysis engine instead of a bounded jumpstart phase.

active_milestone.scope: define the first bounded jumpstart phase for mature repos, including what counts as jumpstart relative to `init` and which one or two surfaces prove visible value first.

immediate_next_action: Archive the completed jumpstart phase and keep the discovery/reporting surfaces as the read path for future mature-repo adoption.
touched_paths: root planning docs and any downstream contract/docs needed after the first slice is shaped
invariants: Jumpstart happens only after safe install/adopt.
The first slice stays bounded and reviewable.
The slice seeds value without pretending to convert the entire repo.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Jumpstart is a post-bootstrap phase, not a more aggressive `init`.
Jumpstart may seed only a small number of clearly high-value surfaces.
The first slice should prefer visible value over completeness.
Broad discovery and ranking stay separate unless they are needed to keep the slice bounded.
completion_criteria: The repo has one checked-in execplan for the bounded mature-repo jumpstart phase.
The plan clearly distinguishes post-bootstrap jumpstart from `init`.
The first visible-value surfaces and the follow-on candidate lanes are explicit enough to guide implementation.

execution_summary.outcome delivered: bounded post-install jumpstart now has a compact discovery report, explicit Memory seed candidates, explicit Planning seed targets, and a fast-payoff ranking rule.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: `uv run pytest tests/test_workspace_cli.py -q`, `uv run ruff check src tests`, `uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py`, and `uv run python scripts/check/check_memory_freshness.py` all passed.
execution_summary.product improvement signal: the jumpstart phase was easier to finish once discovery, memory seeding, planning seeding, and ranking each had their own compact surfaces instead of a single broad post-bootstrap concept.

drift_log: 2026-04-14: Promoted from GitHub issue `#48` after the mature-repo jumpstart tranche was reprioritized ahead of the remaining follow-on items.
2026-04-14: Active plan created to define the first bounded post-install jumpstart phase for mature repos.
